task_completion.description: Agent opens the File menu to initiate the export process.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'File' menu is dropped down in the SolveSpace application.
The 'Export 2D View...' option is highlighted or clicked in the File menu.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the correct target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace' as the current active directory.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the correct filename for the DXF file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename input field in the save dialog contains 'divider.dxf'.

task_completion.description: Agent selects the AutoCAD 2007 DXF format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file type/format dropdown in the save dialog is set to 'AutoCAD 2007 DXF (*.dxf)' or the equivalent DXF option.

task_completion.description: Agent clicks the Save button to finalize the export.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The cursor is clicking the 'Save' button in the export dialog.

integrity.description: Agent used the SolveSpace graphical interface to export the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SolveSpace menus and dialogs, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to fake the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal window is shown executing commands like 'touch divider.dxf' or 'cp'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual SolveSpace application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window clearly displays the SolveSpace interface with the 'divider.slvs' part visible in the background.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'divider.slvs' from the official SolveSpace tutorial. The correct export path is /home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/divider.dxf. The export must be a 2D View in AutoCAD 2007 DXF format, which is a standard option in SolveSpace's 'Export 2D View' dialog.
